mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in real auto sales and housing starts reflect broad-based demand acceleration that lifts aggregate corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases over 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, forcing equity valuations lower and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of tightening-cycle contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth signals stronger macroeconomic momentum that translates into immediate positive market sentiment and short-term S&P 500 price appreciation over the following five trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows or 5-day returns show no significant positive correlation with GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 2.8% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor market inefficiency and hidden weakness, which erodes investor confidence and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a low-rate environment signals accommodative supply-side expansion that quickly boosts short-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of low-rate supply expansion episodes.
